name: Route of the dolmens - circular from Etxarri Arantz (PR-NA 133)
name_original: Ruta de los dólmenes – circular desde Etxarri Arantz (PR-NA 133)
region_intro: Hiking around Etxarri-Aranatz offers access to the diverse landscapes of the Sakana Valley in Navarre, Spain. The region features ancient oak and beech forests, rolling meadows, and significant natural parks like Urbasa and Andia, and Aralar. Hikers can explore varied terrain, from gentle valley paths to more challenging mountain routes, with elevations ranging from low-lying areas to peaks in the surrounding mountain ranges. Best hiking trails around Etxarri-Aranatz The most popular hiking route is Route of the millennial oaks - circular from Etxarri Aranatz (PR-NA 133), a 3.0 miles (4.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 18 minutes to complete. This easy route leads through ancient oak forests with centuries-old trees. Another top favourite among local hikers is Route of the dolmens - circular from Etxarri Arantz (PR-NA 133), a moderate 7.4 miles (11.9 km) path. This trail combines hiking with archaeological interest, passing by ancient megalithic burial sites amidst changing landscapes. Local hikers also love the Maizegur – Fagamendi loop from Etxarri Aranatz, a 9.0 miles (14.4 km) trail leading through varied forest and mountain terrain, often completed in about 5 hours 6 minutes. Hiking around Etxarri-Aranatz is defined by ancient oak and beech forests, megalithic monuments, and views of mountain ranges like Sierra de Andía and Aralar. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Etxarri-Aranatz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 380 reviews. More than 3000 hikers have used komoot to explore Etxarri-Aranatz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Etxarri-Aranatz?
A: There are over 20 hiking trails around Etxarri-Aranatz, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. You'll find options ranging from gentle walks to more challenging mountain ascents.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Etxarri-Aranatz offers 8 easy hiking routes. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970158870/route-of-the-millennial-oaks-circular-from-etxarri-aranatz-pr-na-133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the millennial oaks - circular from Etxarri Aranatz (PR-NA 133)&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.0 miles (4.9 km) long and leads through ancient oak forests, suitable for all fitness levels.

Q: What unique natural features can I expect to see on the trails?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore ancient oak forests with centuries-old trees, discover megalithic monuments like dolmens, and enjoy panoramic views of mountain ranges such as Sierra de Andía and Aralar. The nearby Urbasa and Andia Natural Park is also home to the source of the Urederra River, known for its striking turquoise waters and small waterfalls.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Etxarri-Aranatz?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970158870/route-of-the-millennial-oaks-circular-from-etxarri-aranatz-pr-na-133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the millennial oaks - circular from Etxarri Aranatz (PR-NA 133)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968313696/route-of-the-dolmens-circular-from-etxarri-arantz-pr-na-133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the dolmens - circular from Etxarri Arantz (PR-NA 133)&lt;/a&gt;, both offering a complete loop experience.

Q: What kind of historical or archaeological sites can be found along the hiking paths?
A: The region is notable for its megalithic monuments. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968313696/route-of-the-dolmens-circular-from-etxarri-arantz-pr-na-133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the dolmens - circular from Etxarri Arantz (PR-NA 133)&lt;/a&gt; specifically leads past ancient burial sites like the Beitzeta, Iruiturrieta, Jentiltzulo, Fagamendi, and Maitzagur dolmens, set amidst diverse landscapes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Etxarri-Aranatz?
A: The routes in Etxarri-Aranatz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 380 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse ecosystems, from ancient oak forests to beechwoods, and the well-maintained trail network.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced mountaineers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers, there are 3 difficult routes available. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5345694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maizegur – Fagamendi loop from Etxarri Aranatz&lt;/a&gt;, a 9.0 miles (14.4 km) trail leading through varied forest and mountain terrain with significant elevation gain.

Q: What are some notable peaks or viewpoints accessible from Etxarri-Aranatz?
A: You can find several impressive peaks and viewpoints. Mount Beriain offers spectacular views and includes a visit to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3379327" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Donato&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable summits include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3426453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bargagain Summit&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5548584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Donato Summit&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through mountain passes?
A: Yes, the region features several mountain passes that can be incorporated into hikes. You might encounter passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3083575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lizarrusti Pass&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5136015" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Lizarraga&lt;/a&gt;, or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3873746" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Unanu Pass&lt;/a&gt;, offering varied terrain and scenic views.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Etxarri-Aranatz?
A: Etxarri-Aranatz offers hiking opportunities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, with changing foliage in the forests. Summer is also popular, though some higher routes might be warmer. Winter hiking is possible, especially on lower elevation trails, but requires appropriate gear and checking local conditions.

Q: Are there any long-distance trails connected to Etxarri-Aranatz?
A: Yes, Etxarri-Aranatz is connected to longer-distance paths. These include the Euskal Herria Footpath (GR 12), the Idiazabal Cheese Route (GR 283), and the Shepherd's Path (GR 282), allowing for multi-day trekking adventures for those seeking extended journeys.
tips: This picturesque path will take you through ancient beech forests visiting several dolmens that give the name to the route. A path to walk with the whole family and enjoy at any time of the year.

One of the dolmens that are on the path, surrounded by a wonderful beech forest that offers a unique landscape that varies depending on the time of year you visit. In the autumn months you will have intense contrasts worthy of postcards.
A beautiful and picturesque landscape offered by this beech forest that has ancient specimens. The landscape in autumn is simply wonderful for its intense and varied colors.
